feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core can operate at 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C speed.
feature_list.test_constraints: Prescale/configuration method not fully specified in this requirement; verify observable SCL timing only.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer and observe SCL/SDA timing.
Measure the resulting bus bit rate.
expected_results: I2C transfer occurs with SCL corresponding to 100 Kbps operation.
Normal-mode 100 Kbps operation is supported by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core can operate at 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C speed.
feature_list.test_constraints: Prescale/configuration method not fully specified in this requirement; verify observable SCL timing only.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer and observe SCL/SDA timing.
Measure the resulting bus bit rate.
expected_results: I2C transfer occurs with SCL corresponding to 400 Kbps operation.
Fast-mode 400 Kbps operation is supported by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the exposed host interface signals match the defined WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_constraints: Protocol compliance details beyond listed signals are not fully specified here.
test_steps: Inspect and connect the WISHBONE interface signals listed for the core.
Perform basic read and write accesses using wb_clk_i, wb_rst_i, wb_adr_i, wb_dat_i, wb_dat_o, wb_we_i, wb_stb_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_ack_o.
Observe wb_inta_o availability during interrupt-related operation.
expected_results: The listed WISHBONE interface signals are present and usable for basic bus accesses.
Basic interface behavior is consistent with a WISHBONE Classic host interface.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access completes in two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Assumes a valid WISHBONE access handshake is applied.
test_steps: Start a valid WISHBONE read or write access with wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o asserts.
Repeat for both read and write accesses.
expected_results: wb_ack_o asserts on the second clock cycle of the access.
Each read and write access takes two clock cycles.

feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level must be configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reset operates as active high when ARST_LVL is set to active high.
feature_list.test_constraints: ARST_LVL configured to 1’b1.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL for active-high reset.
Assert arst_i high and observe core reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and observe release from reset.
expected_results: The core resets when arst_i is high.
The core releases from reset when arst_i is deasserted.

feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level must be configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reset operates as active low when ARST_LVL is set to active low.
feature_list.test_constraints: ARST_LVL configured to 1’b0.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL for active-low reset.
Assert arst_i low and observe core reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and observe release from reset.
expected_results: The core resets when arst_i is low.
The core releases from reset when arst_i is deasserted.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: None specified.
test_steps: Inspect the external interface of the core.
Confirm presence of scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, scl_pad_oe, sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
Exercise basic bus activity and observe these signals toggle/respond.
expected_results: All six pad interface signals are present.
The signals participate in external bus connection behavior.

feature_list.requirement_text: Devices connected to SCL and SDA must use open-drain or open-collector outputs.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the external SCL and SDA connection uses open-drain/open-collector style signaling.
feature_list.test_constraints: Depends on external integration and board connection.
test_steps: Connect the core to an open-drain/open-collector style SCL/SDA bus.
Run bus activity and observe line behavior when devices release the bus.
Confirm the lines are not actively driven high by connected devices.
expected_results: SCL and SDA bus behavior is consistent with open-drain/open-collector usage.
Connected devices release the bus for logic high.

feature_list.requirement_text: The SCL and SDA output-enable signals must control whether the core drives or releases the external bus lines.
feature_list.test_description: Verify scl_pad_oe controls whether the core drives or releases SCL.
feature_list.test_constraints: Connection is made per the external connection figure; exact electrical polarity of drive value is not explicitly stated.
test_steps: Cause the core to assert and deassert scl_pad_oe during bus activity.
Observe scl_pad_o, scl_pad_oe, and the external SCL line.
Compare line behavior between enabled and released states.
expected_results: When scl_pad_oe indicates drive, the core controls SCL.
When scl_pad_oe indicates release, the core releases SCL to the external bus.

feature_list.requirement_text: The SCL and SDA output-enable signals must control whether the core drives or releases the external bus lines.
feature_list.test_description: Verify sda_pad_oe controls whether the core drives or releases SDA.
feature_list.test_constraints: Connection is made per the external connection figure; exact electrical polarity of drive value is not explicitly stated.
test_steps: Cause the core to assert and deassert sda_pad_oe during bus activity.
Observe sda_pad_o, sda_pad_oe, and the external SDA line.
Compare line behavior between enabled and released states.
expected_results: When sda_pad_oe indicates drive, the core controls SDA.
When sda_pad_oe indicates release, the core releases SDA to the external bus.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERlo must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale low-byte register at address 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo is an 8-bit read/write register at address 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: None specified.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit test value to address 0x00.
Read back from address 0x00.
Compare readback to the written value.
expected_results: Address 0x00 accepts an 8-bit write.
Readback from address 0x00 returns the written PRERlo value.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERhi must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale high-byte register at address 0x01.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERhi is an 8-bit read/write register at address 0x01.
feature_list.test_constraints: None specified.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit test value to address 0x01.
Read back from address 0x01.
Compare readback to the written value.
expected_results: Address 0x01 accepts an 8-bit write.
Readback from address 0x01 returns the written PRERhi value.

feature_list.requirement_text: CTR must be an 8-bit read/write Control register at address 0x02.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR is an 8-bit read/write register at address 0x02.
feature_list.test_constraints: Reserved bits guidance exists separately; use defined writable bits only where possible.
test_steps: Write a control value to address 0x02.
Read back from address 0x02.
Compare defined read/write bits with the written value.
expected_results: Address 0x02 accepts an 8-bit control write.
Readback from address 0x02 reflects the control register value for defined RW bits.

feature_list.requirement_text: TXR must be an 8-bit write-only Transmit register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR is an 8-bit write-only register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_constraints: Write-only behavior means direct readback is not required by this requirement.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit test value to address 0x03 with wb_we_i asserted.
Use the written value in a transmit-related operation.
Observe that the core accepts the TXR write.
expected_results: Address 0x03 accepts an 8-bit write as TXR.
The written transmit value is used by subsequent transmit behavior.

feature_list.requirement_text: RXR must be an 8-bit read-only Receive register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR is an 8-bit read-only register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a received byte to populate RXR.
test_steps: Cause the core to receive one byte via I2C.
Read address 0x03 with wb_we_i deasserted.
Observe the returned receive data.
expected_results: Address 0x03 returns an 8-bit receive value as RXR on read.
The read value corresponds to the last received byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: CR must be an 8-bit write-only Command register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR is an 8-bit write-only register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_constraints: Write-only behavior means direct readback is not required by this requirement.
test_steps: Write a command value to address 0x04 with wb_we_i asserted.
Observe command-related core behavior after the write.
Confirm the access is accepted as a command write.
expected_results: Address 0x04 accepts an 8-bit write as CR.
The written command affects core operation.

feature_list.requirement_text: SR must be an 8-bit read-only Status register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR is an 8-bit read-only register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a known status condition for meaningful observation.
test_steps: Place the core in a known status condition.
Read address 0x04 with wb_we_i deasserted.
Observe the returned status value.
expected_results: Address 0x04 returns an 8-bit status value as SR on read.
The read value reflects the current status condition.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register must configure the SCL clock using the WISHBONE input clock and the 5SCL prescale formula.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo/PRERhi program the SCL clock according to the 5*SCL frequency minus 1 relationship to wb_clk_i.
feature_list.test_constraints: Exact numeric formula wording is limited to '5*SCL frequency (minus 1)'; test via representative prescale settings.
test_steps: Program PRERlo/PRERhi for a target SCL rate based on wb_clk_i and the 5*SCL minus 1 rule.
Initiate an I2C transfer.
Measure SCL and compare it with the programmed expectation.
expected_results: Changing the Prescale Register changes SCL frequency.
Observed SCL corresponds to the wb_clk_i-derived 5*SCL prescale programming rule.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register value must only be changed when the EN bit is cleared.
feature_list.test_description: Verify prescale changes are only permitted when EN is cleared.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement is phrased as a usage rule; hardware enforcement is not explicitly defined.
test_steps: Set EN to 1 and attempt to change PRERlo/PRERhi.
Repeat with EN set to 0 and change PRERlo/PRERhi.
Observe whether only the EN=0 case is valid/supported.
expected_results: Prescale update with EN=0 is accepted as the valid programming condition.
Behavior for EN=1 is not fully defined, so enforcement can only be assessed partially.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register reset value must be 0xFFFF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRER reset value is 0xFFFF.
feature_list.test_constraints: Apply reset before any register writes.
test_steps: Assert and release reset.
Read PRERlo at 0x00 and PRERhi at 0x01.
Combine the two bytes into the prescale value.
expected_results: PRERlo reads 0xFF and PRERhi reads 0xFF after reset.
Combined Prescale Register reset value is 0xFFFF.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 7 must be the read/write EN bit used to enable or disable the I2C core.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 7 enables the core when written to 1.
feature_list.test_constraints: None specified.
test_steps: Write CTR bit 7 = 1.
Read back CTR.
Observe the EN bit state.
expected_results: CTR bit 7 stores as 1.
The core is in enabled state when EN=1.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 7 must be the read/write EN bit used to enable or disable the I2C core.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 7 disables the core when written to 0.
feature_list.test_constraints: None specified.
test_steps: Write CTR bit 7 = 0.
Read back CTR.
Observe the EN bit state.
expected_results: CTR bit 7 stores as 0.
The core is in disabled state when EN=0.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must only respond to new commands when EN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core responds to a new command when EN is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a valid command such as WR or RD.
test_steps: Set EN to 1.
Write a valid new command to CR.
Observe command execution by core status or bus activity.
expected_results: The core accepts and executes the new command when EN=1.
Observable status or bus activity changes occur.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must only respond to new commands when EN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core does not respond to a new command when EN is cleared.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use the same valid command as the positive case.
test_steps: Set EN to 0.
Write a valid new command to CR.
Observe command execution by core status or bus activity.
expected_results: The core does not respond to the new command when EN=0.
No corresponding command-driven status or bus activity occurs.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software must only clear EN when no transfer is in progress, after a STOP command, or when STO is set.
feature_list.test_description: Assess clearing EN outside the allowed software conditions.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement is a software usage rule; hardware enforcement is not explicitly defined.
test_steps: Attempt to clear EN while a transfer is in progress and STO is not set.
Attempt to clear EN after STOP or with STO set.
Compare observed behavior.
expected_results: Clearing EN after STOP or with STO set is the valid documented condition.
Enforcement of invalid timing is not fully defined by the requirement.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 6 must be the read/write IEN bit used to enable or disable interrupt generation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 6 enables interrupt generation when written to 1.
feature_list.test_constraints: A pending interrupt condition is needed to observe wb_inta_o effect.
test_steps: Write CTR bit 6 = 1.
Create a pending interrupt condition.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: CTR bit 6 stores as 1.
Interrupt generation is enabled when IEN=1.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 6 must be the read/write IEN bit used to enable or disable interrupt generation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 6 disables interrupt generation when written to 0.
feature_list.test_constraints: A pending interrupt condition is needed to observe wb_inta_o effect.
test_steps: Write CTR bit 6 = 0.
Create a pending interrupt condition.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: CTR bit 6 stores as 0.
Interrupt generation is disabled when IEN=0.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Control Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: Apply reset before any register writes.
test_steps: Assert and release reset.
Read CTR at address 0x02.
expected_results: CTR reads 0x00 after reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register must hold the byte to transmit, with bit 0 acting as the data LSB during data transfer or the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR[7:0] holds the transmitted data byte during data transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a data transfer, not an address transfer.
test_steps: Write a known data byte to TXR.
Initiate a write data transfer.
Observe the transmitted SDA bits.
expected_results: The transmitted byte matches TXR[7:0].
TXR bit 0 is transmitted as the data LSB in data transfer.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register must hold the byte to transmit, with bit 0 acting as the data LSB during data transfer or the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR bit 0 acts as the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a slave address transfer.
test_steps: Write a slave address byte into TXR with bit 0 set to the desired RW value.
Initiate the address transfer.
Observe the transmitted address byte on SDA.
expected_results: The first transmitted byte matches TXR contents for the address transfer.
TXR bit 0 is used as the RW bit during slave address transfer.

feature_list.requirement_text: During a slave address transfer, RW value 1 must indicate reading from the slave and RW value 0 must indicate writing to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RW=1 indicates reading from the slave during address transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a slave address transfer with TXR[0]=1.
test_steps: Load TXR with a slave address byte having RW=1.
Initiate the slave address transfer.
Observe subsequent transfer direction.
expected_results: The address byte is transmitted with RW=1.
The transfer direction is read from slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: During a slave address transfer, RW value 1 must indicate reading from the slave and RW value 0 must indicate writing to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RW=0 indicates writing to the slave during address transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a slave address transfer with TXR[0]=0.
test_steps: Load TXR with a slave address byte having RW=0.
Initiate the slave address transfer.
Observe subsequent transfer direction.
expected_results: The address byte is transmitted with RW=0.
The transfer direction is write to slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: TXR is write-only; direct readback is not defined.
test_steps: Assert and release reset.
Use TXR in the first transmit operation without writing a new value.
Observe the transmitted byte if accessible.
expected_results: TXR reset state is consistent with 0x00.
Direct verification is limited because TXR is write-only.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain the last byte received via I2C.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR contains the last byte received via I2C.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a known received data byte.
test_steps: Cause the core to receive a known byte via I2C.
Read RXR at address 0x03.
Compare RXR to the received byte.
expected_results: RXR contains the last byte received via I2C.
The read value matches the received byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: Apply reset before any receive activity.
test_steps: Assert and release reset.
Read RXR at address 0x03.
expected_results: RXR reads 0x00 after reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 7 must be the write-only STA bit used to generate a START or Repeated START condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writing CR bit 7 requests START or Repeated START generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Combine with a valid command context as required by protocol behavior.
test_steps: Write STA in CR during an idle or active transfer context.
Observe bus activity on SDA and SCL.
Check for START-form bus condition generation.
expected_results: Writing STA causes generation of a START-family condition.
The command bit functions as the START/Re-START request bit.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 6 must be the write-only STO bit used to generate a STOP condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writing CR bit 6 requests STOP generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use an active transfer context.
test_steps: Write STO in CR.
Observe bus activity on SDA and SCL.
Check for STOP-form bus condition generation.
expected_results: Writing STO causes generation of a STOP condition.
The command bit functions as the STOP request bit.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 5 must be the write-only RD bit used to initiate a read from the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writing CR bit 5 initiates a read from the slave.
feature_list.test_constraints: A valid addressed slave read context is required.
test_steps: Place the core in a valid read context.
Write RD in CR.
Observe receive activity and status progression.
expected_results: Writing RD initiates a slave read operation.
The core proceeds with receive behavior.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 4 must be the write-only WR bit used to initiate a write to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writing CR bit 4 initiates a write to the slave.
feature_list.test_constraints: A valid addressed slave write context is required.
test_steps: Load TXR with a transmit byte.
Write WR in CR.
Observe transmit activity and status progression.
expected_results: Writing WR initiates a slave write operation.
The core proceeds with transmit behavior.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 3 must be the write-only ACK bit used by a receiver to send ACK when 0 or NACK when 1.
feature_list.test_description: Verify ACK=0 causes the receiver to send ACK.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a receiver context.
test_steps: Set up a read/receive operation.
Write CR with ACK bit = 0 in the receiver command.
Observe the acknowledge bit on SDA on the ninth clock.
expected_results: The receiver sends ACK when ACK=0.
The ninth clock acknowledge bit is low.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 3 must be the write-only ACK bit used by a receiver to send ACK when 0 or NACK when 1.
feature_list.test_description: Verify ACK=1 causes the receiver to send NACK.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a receiver context.
test_steps: Set up a read/receive operation.
Write CR with ACK bit = 1 in the receiver command.
Observe the acknowledge bit on SDA on the ninth clock.
expected_results: The receiver sends NACK when ACK=1.
The ninth clock acknowledge bit is high/released.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 0 must be the write-only IACK bit used to clear a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writing IACK clears a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_constraints: A pending interrupt must exist before the test.
test_steps: Create a pending interrupt and confirm IF is set.
Write IACK in CR.
Observe IF and wb_inta_o.
expected_results: The pending interrupt is cleared by IACK.
IF clears and interrupt request deasserts if present.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STA clears automatically and reads back as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Command register is write-only in the register map; read-as-zero behavior may need indirect/read path confirmation if available.
test_steps: Write STA in CR.
Allow command processing to proceed.
Read command register path if available or inspect command bit state indirectly.
expected_results: STA clears automatically after command acceptance.
STA reads as zero where readable behavior is observable.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STO clears automatically and reads back as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Command register is write-only in the register map; read-as-zero behavior may need indirect/read path confirmation if available.
test_steps: Write STO in CR.
Allow command processing to proceed.
Read command register path if available or inspect command bit state indirectly.
expected_results: STO clears automatically after command acceptance.
STO reads as zero where readable behavior is observable.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RD clears automatically and reads back as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Command register is write-only in the register map; read-as-zero behavior may need indirect/read path confirmation if available.
test_steps: Write RD in CR.
Allow command processing to proceed.
Read command register path if available or inspect command bit state indirectly.
expected_results: RD clears automatically after command acceptance.
RD reads as zero where readable behavior is observable.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify WR clears automatically and reads back as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Command register is write-only in the register map; read-as-zero behavior may need indirect/read path confirmation if available.
test_steps: Write WR in CR.
Allow command processing to proceed.
Read command register path if available or inspect command bit state indirectly.
expected_results: WR clears automatically after command acceptance.
WR reads as zero where readable behavior is observable.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IACK clears automatically and reads back as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Command register is write-only in the register map; read-as-zero behavior may need indirect/read path confirmation if available.
test_steps: Write IACK in CR.
Allow command processing to proceed.
Read command register path if available or inspect command bit state indirectly.
expected_results: IACK clears automatically after command acceptance.
IACK reads as zero where readable behavior is observable.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Command Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: CR is write-only; direct readback is not defined.
test_steps: Assert and release reset.
Observe that no command-driven action occurs until a command is written.
expected_results: CR reset state is consistent with 0x00.
No command bits are active after reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: RxACK must indicate whether acknowledge was received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RxACK=0 indicates acknowledge received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a slave that acknowledges.
test_steps: Address a slave that acknowledges.
Complete the address or write phase.
Read SR bit 7.
expected_results: RxACK reads 0 when acknowledge is received.
Status reflects slave acknowledgment.

feature_list.requirement_text: RxACK must indicate whether acknowledge was received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RxACK=1 indicates no acknowledge received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a NACK condition from the addressed or non-responding slave.
test_steps: Issue an address or write phase that results in no acknowledge.
Complete the transfer phase.
Read SR bit 7.
expected_results: RxACK reads 1 when no acknowledge is received.
Status reflects missing slave acknowledgment.

feature_list.requirement_text: BUSY must indicate whether the I2C bus is busy, setting after START detection and clearing after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify BUSY sets after START detection.
feature_list.test_constraints: Generate a START condition on the bus.
test_steps: Generate a START condition.
Read SR bit 6 after START detection.
expected_results: BUSY reads 1 after START detection.

feature_list.requirement_text: BUSY must indicate whether the I2C bus is busy, setting after START detection and clearing after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify BUSY clears after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_constraints: Generate a STOP condition after bus activity.
test_steps: Generate a STOP condition.
Read SR bit 6 after STOP detection.
expected_results: BUSY reads 0 after STOP detection.

feature_list.requirement_text: AL must indicate arbitration lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL sets when arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a multi-master or forced arbitration-loss condition.
test_steps: Create a bus scenario in which the core loses arbitration.
Read SR bit 5.
Observe arbitration-loss status.
expected_results: AL sets when arbitration is lost.
Status indicates arbitration loss.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when a STOP signal is detected but was not requested.
feature_list.test_description: Verify unrequested STOP detection causes arbitration lost.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a scenario where STOP is observed while STO was not requested.
test_steps: Create bus activity where a STOP condition is detected without the core requesting STO.
Read AL status after the STOP event.
expected_results: AL sets when an unrequested STOP is detected.
The core treats the event as arbitration lost.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when the master drives SDA high but SDA is low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify arbitration lost when the core drives SDA high but observes SDA low.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a conflicting bus driver or forced line condition.
test_steps: Force a condition where the core releases/drives SDA high while sda_pad_i is low.
Continue the bus bit evaluation.
Read AL status.
expected_results: AL sets when driven/high-intended SDA is observed low.
The core treats the mismatch as arbitration lost.

feature_list.requirement_text: TIP must indicate whether a transfer is in progress.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TIP=1 while transferring data.
feature_list.test_constraints: A data transfer must be active.
test_steps: Initiate a data transfer.
Read SR bit 1 during the transfer.
expected_results: TIP reads 1 while transfer is in progress.

feature_list.requirement_text: TIP must indicate whether a transfer is in progress.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TIP=0 when transfer is complete.
feature_list.test_constraints: Allow the transfer to complete.
test_steps: Complete a data transfer.
Read SR bit 1 after completion.
expected_results: TIP reads 0 when transfer is complete.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must indicate whether an interrupt is pending.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF indicates a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_constraints: Create any condition that sets the interrupt flag.
test_steps: Create a condition that makes an interrupt pending.
Read SR bit 0.
expected_results: IF reads 1 when an interrupt is pending.

feature_list.requirement_text: A pending interrupt must cause a processor interrupt request when IEN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a pending interrupt asserts wb_inta_o when IEN is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: IEN must be set and IF must become pending.
test_steps: Set IEN to 1.
Create a pending interrupt condition.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: wb_inta_o asserts when IF is pending and IEN=1.

feature_list.requirement_text: A pending interrupt must cause a processor interrupt request when IEN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a pending interrupt does not assert wb_inta_o when IEN is cleared.
feature_list.test_constraints: IEN must be cleared while IF becomes pending.
test_steps: Set IEN to 0.
Create a pending interrupt condition.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: wb_inta_o does not assert when IF is pending and IEN=0.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must be set when one byte transfer has completed or arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF sets when one byte transfer completes.
feature_list.test_constraints: Perform any valid one-byte transfer.
test_steps: Execute a one-byte transfer to completion.
Read SR bit 0 after completion.
expected_results: IF sets when one byte transfer has completed.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must be set when one byte transfer has completed or arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF sets when arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires an arbitration-loss condition.
test_steps: Create an arbitration-loss event.
Read SR bit 0 after the event.
expected_results: IF sets when arbitration is lost.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Status Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: Apply reset before any bus activity.
test_steps: Assert and release reset.
Read SR at address 0x04.
expected_results: SR reads 0x00 after reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reserved status bits SR[4:2] read as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: None specified.
test_steps: Read SR under representative operating conditions.
Check bits SR[4:2].
expected_results: SR reserved bits [4:2] read as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reserved command bits CR[2:1] read as zero where observable.
feature_list.test_constraints: CR is write-only in the register map; read-as-zero observability is limited.
test_steps: Attempt to observe command register read behavior if available.
Check bits CR[2:1] on any readable path.
expected_results: Reserved command bits read as zero where readable behavior is observable.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writable reserved control bits should be written as zero during software access.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is software guidance; hardware enforcement is not defined.
test_steps: Write CTR using only defined control bits with reserved bits held at zero.
Read back CTR and inspect reserved bit behavior if readable.
expected_results: Using zero for writable reserved control bits is the documented valid programming practice.
Any hardware enforcement of non-zero writes is not fully defined.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data must be transferred between a Master and Slave synchronously to SCL on SDA, byte by byte.
feature_list.test_description: Verify I2C data transfer occurs synchronously to SCL on SDA and is byte-based.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a representative master-slave transfer.
test_steps: Run a transfer between the core and a slave.
Observe SDA transitions relative to SCL across one or more bytes.
Group transferred bits into byte units.
expected_results: Data on SDA is transferred synchronously with SCL.
Transfer occurs on a byte-by-byte basis.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each I2C data byte must be 8 bits long and transmitted most significant bit first.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each transferred data byte is 8 bits long.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a complete byte transfer.
test_steps: Initiate a byte transfer.
Count SCL pulses associated with the data bits before acknowledge.
expected_results: Each data byte consists of 8 data bits.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each I2C data byte must be 8 bits long and transmitted most significant bit first.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data is transmitted most significant bit first.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a non-symmetric test byte.
test_steps: Load or receive a known byte with distinguishable bit order.
Observe SDA across the 8 data bit times.
Compare observed order to the expected bit sequence.
expected_results: The most significant bit is transmitted first.
Observed bit order matches MSB-first transmission.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each transferred byte must be followed by an acknowledge bit on the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each transferred byte is followed by an acknowledge bit on the ninth SCL clock.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a complete byte transfer including acknowledge phase.
test_steps: Execute a byte transfer.
Count SCL cycles for the 8 data bits and the following acknowledge phase.
Observe SDA on the ninth clock cycle.
expected_results: An acknowledge bit follows each transferred byte.
The acknowledge phase occurs on the ninth SCL clock cycle.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data bits must be sampled during SCL high, and SDA must remain stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data bits are sampled during SCL high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe normal data transfer timing.
test_steps: Run a data transfer.
Observe the data sampling point relative to SCL.
Correlate valid data with SCL high periods.
expected_results: Data bits are sampled during SCL high.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data bits must be sampled during SCL high, and SDA must remain stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA remains stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe normal data transfer timing.
test_steps: Run a data transfer.
Observe SDA while SCL is high across data bits.
expected_results: SDA remains stable during the high period of SCL for data bits.

feature_list.requirement_text: A standard I2C communication must follow the sequence START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a standard communication follows START, address, data, STOP sequence.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a standard single transaction.
test_steps: Initiate a normal I2C transaction.
Observe the bus sequence from beginning to end.
Check the ordering of START, address, data, and STOP.
expected_results: The communication sequence is START, slave address transfer, data transfer, then STOP.

feature_list.requirement_text: A START signal must be a high-to-low transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START is defined by SDA high-to-low while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a generated START condition.
test_steps: Generate or capture a START condition.
Observe SDA and SCL at the START instant.
expected_results: START corresponds to SDA transitioning high-to-low while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: A Repeated START must be a START signal generated without first generating a STOP signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify Repeated START occurs without a preceding STOP.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a transfer that issues a repeated START.
test_steps: Generate a START followed by bus activity, then request another START without STOP.
Observe the bus conditions between the two START events.
expected_results: A second START is generated without an intervening STOP.
The bus condition is a Repeated START.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must generate a START signal when STA is set and either RD or WR is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START is generated when STA and RD are set.
feature_list.test_constraints: EN should be set so commands are accepted.
test_steps: Write a command with STA and RD set.
Observe SDA and SCL for START generation.
expected_results: The core generates a START signal when STA and RD are set.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must generate a START signal when STA is set and either RD or WR is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START is generated when STA and WR are set.
feature_list.test_constraints: EN should be set so commands are accepted.
test_steps: Write a command with STA and WR set.
Observe SDA and SCL for START generation.
expected_results: The core generates a START signal when STA and WR are set.

feature_list.requirement_text: A STOP signal must be a low-to-high transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STOP is defined by SDA low-to-high while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a generated STOP condition.
test_steps: Generate or capture a STOP condition.
Observe SDA and SCL at the STOP instant.
expected_results: STOP corresponds to SDA transitioning low-to-high while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: The first byte transferred by the master after a START signal must be the slave address with a seven-bit address followed by an RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the first byte after START is a 7-bit slave address followed by RW.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a 7-bit address transaction.
test_steps: Initiate a transaction with START.
Observe the first transmitted byte after START.
Decode the byte fields.
expected_results: The first byte after START is the slave address byte.
The byte contains 7 address bits followed by the RW bit.

feature_list.requirement_text: The addressed slave must acknowledge by pulling SDA low at the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the addressed slave acknowledges by pulling SDA low on the ninth SCL clock.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a matching addressed slave that acknowledges.
test_steps: Transmit a slave address to a matching slave.
Observe SDA during the ninth SCL pulse.
Check the acknowledge level.
expected_results: The addressed slave pulls SDA low on the ninth SCL clock cycle.
An acknowledge is observed.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must support 10-bit slave addresses by generating two address transfers.
feature_list.test_description: Verify 10-bit addressing is supported by generating two address transfers.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a 10-bit address transaction.
test_steps: Initiate a 10-bit slave address transfer.
Observe the address phase on the bus.
Count the number of address transfers generated.
expected_results: The core generates two address transfers for a 10-bit slave address.
10-bit addressing is supported.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must treat a slave address transfer as a write action using the Transmit Register and WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave address transfer is performed by loading TXR and setting WR.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use any slave address transfer.
test_steps: Load the slave address byte into TXR.
Set WR to start the transfer.
Observe the address byte transmitted on the bus.
expected_results: The core uses TXR contents for the slave address transfer.
Setting WR causes the address byte to be transferred.

feature_list.requirement_text: After successful slave addressing, data transfer must proceed byte by byte in the direction specified by the RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data transfer proceeds byte by byte in read direction after successful addressing.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use successful addressing with RW indicating read.
test_steps: Perform successful slave addressing with RW set for read.
Continue the transaction.
Observe byte-by-byte data transfer direction.
expected_results: After successful addressing, data transfers proceed byte by byte.
Transfer direction follows RW=read.

feature_list.requirement_text: After successful slave addressing, data transfer must proceed byte by byte in the direction specified by the RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data transfer proceeds byte by byte in write direction after successful addressing.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use successful addressing with RW indicating write.
test_steps: Perform successful slave addressing with RW set for write.
Continue the transaction.
Observe byte-by-byte data transfer direction.
expected_results: After successful addressing, data transfers proceed byte by byte.
Transfer direction follows RW=write.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave signals No Acknowledge, the master may generate a STOP or Repeated START signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a STOP may be generated after slave No Acknowledge.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a NACK condition.
test_steps: Create a transfer where the slave signals No Acknowledge.
Issue STO after the NACK.
Observe the bus.
expected_results: The master can generate a STOP after No Acknowledge.
The transfer is aborted with STOP.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave signals No Acknowledge, the master may generate a STOP or Repeated START signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a Repeated START may be generated after slave No Acknowledge.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a NACK condition.
test_steps: Create a transfer where the slave signals No Acknowledge.
Issue STA for a repeated START after the NACK.
Observe the bus.
expected_results: The master can generate a Repeated START after No Acknowledge.
A new transfer cycle can begin without STOP.

feature_list.requirement_text: To write data to a slave, software must store the data in the Transmit Register and set the WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify write data transfer is initiated by loading TXR and setting WR.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a valid addressed slave write context.
test_steps: Write a data byte into TXR.
Set WR in CR.
Observe the data byte transmitted on the bus.
expected_results: The core transmits the TXR data byte after WR is set.
This is the documented write-data sequence.

feature_list.requirement_text: To read data from a slave, software must set the RD bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify read data transfer is initiated by setting RD.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a valid addressed slave read context.
test_steps: Set RD in CR.
Observe receive activity and data capture.
Read RXR after completion if applicable.
expected_results: The core initiates a read from the slave when RD is set.
Receive activity occurs and data is captured.

feature_list.requirement_text: When a transfer is done, the core must reset TIP and set IF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer completion clears TIP and sets IF.
feature_list.test_constraints: Complete any valid transfer.
test_steps: Execute a transfer to completion.
Read SR after the transfer completes.
expected_results: TIP is cleared when the transfer is done.
IF is set when the transfer is done.

feature_list.requirement_text: When interrupt generation is enabled and a transfer is done, the core must generate an interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer completion generates an interrupt when IEN is enabled.
feature_list.test_constraints: IEN must be set before completing the transfer.
test_steps: Set IEN to 1.
Execute a transfer to completion.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: An interrupt is generated when the transfer completes and IEN is enabled.

feature_list.requirement_text: When interrupt generation is enabled and a transfer is done, the core must generate an interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer completion does not generate a processor interrupt when IEN is disabled.
feature_list.test_constraints: IEN must be 0 before completing the transfer.
test_steps: Set IEN to 0.
Execute a transfer to completion.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: No processor interrupt request is generated when the transfer completes and IEN is disabled.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain valid data after IF has been set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR contains valid data after IF is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a read transfer that produces received data.
test_steps: Execute a read transfer until IF sets.
Read RXR after IF is set.
Compare RXR with the received bus byte.
expected_results: RXR contains valid received data after IF is set.
The RXR value matches the completed receive byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software may issue a new read or write command when TIP is reset.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a new read or write command may be issued when TIP is reset.
feature_list.test_constraints: Wait until no transfer is in progress.
test_steps: Execute a transfer and wait for TIP to become 0.
Issue a new RD or WR command.
Observe that the new command is accepted.
expected_results: A new read or write command can be issued when TIP=0.
The core accepts the new command.

feature_list.requirement_text: When two or more masters try to control the bus simultaneously, clock synchronization must determine the bus clock.
feature_list.test_description: Verify simultaneous multi-master control results in clock synchronization determining the bus clock.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a multi-master bus environment.
test_steps: Connect two masters that attempt bus control simultaneously.
Observe the resulting SCL waveform on the shared bus.
Compare the final bus clock with the masters' individual timing intents.
expected_results: A clock synchronization procedure determines the resulting bus clock under simultaneous master control.

feature_list.requirement_text: During clock synchronization, SCL must be held low by the device with the longest low period and held high by the device with the shortest high period.
feature_list.test_description: Verify synchronized SCL low period follows the longest low period and high period follows the shortest high period.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a multi-master bus with differing SCL timing.
test_steps: Run two masters with different SCL low and high periods simultaneously.
Observe the shared SCL waveform.
Compare bus low and high durations with each master's timing.
expected_results: The shared SCL low period follows the longest master low period.
The shared SCL high period follows the shortest master high period.

feature_list.requirement_text: Slave devices may slow the transfer bit rate by holding SCL low after the master has driven SCL low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a slave can hold SCL low after the master drives SCL low.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a slave capable of clock stretching.
test_steps: Start a transfer with a clock-stretching slave.
Observe SCL after the master drives it low.
Check whether the slave continues holding SCL low before release.
expected_results: The slave can hold SCL low after the master drives SCL low.
The transfer bit rate is slowed by the slave action.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave SCL low period is greater than the master SCL low period, the resulting SCL bus low period must be stretched and wait states inserted.
feature_list.test_description: Verify longer slave SCL low period stretches the bus low period and inserts wait states.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a slave that holds SCL low longer than the master's programmed low period.
test_steps: Run a transfer with a clock-stretching slave whose SCL low period exceeds the master's.
Observe the shared SCL low period.
Compare the transfer timing against the master's unstretched timing.
expected_results: The SCL bus low period is stretched to the longer slave low period.
Wait states are inserted into the transfer.
